page: Microsoft Azure D48ps_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dpsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture powered by a Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor with 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The processor configuration includes a 64 KB L1 data cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 128 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

In benchmark evaluations, the D48ps_v6 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory performance. The multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests place the instance in the strong and top-tier categories respectively, highlighting the efficiency of its 48 dedicated physical cores. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with uncached read and write large-block operations reaching top-tier performance levels at 464.92 GB/sec and 173.04 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET benchmarks also show strong to top-tier results, with Redis handling 89.47 million operations per second. However, single-core performance in Geekbench and stress-ng remains in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture provides high resource density and excellent multi-threaded performance per unit of resource, making it a viable alternative to traditional x86 architectures for scale-out workloads. The lack of bundled local storage and public IPv4 addresses means storage and networking costs are decoupled, allowing for precise resource allocation. This server is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM text generation tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ps_v6 is an ARM64-based general-purpose virtual machine from the Dpsv6 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 48 dedicated physical cores (48 vCPUs) powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core). The instance lacks bundled local storage and integrated GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks reveal top-tier memory bandwidth, with uncached read speeds of 464.92 GB/sec and write speeds of 173.04 GB/sec, alongside strong multi-core processing and database performance. While single-core performance is average, its high multi-threaded efficiency makes it highly cost-effective for scale-out workloads. It is best suited for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and medium-model LLM text generation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ps_v6 is an ARM64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 192 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ps_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 48 dedicated cores, 192 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine delivering dedicated multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth for scale-out workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: ARM64 architecture with 48 dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 cores at 3.4 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth with 464.92 GB/sec read and 173.04 GB/sec write speeds
Strong multi-core performance for database operations and software compilation
No bundled local storage, offering flexibility via Premium Storage compatibility
Optimized for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and scale-out workloads
